planted.note: No stop conditions — agent never escalates

findings.key: eval::no human baseline to beat — you cannot tell if the agent is an improvement.
findings.claim: No human baseline to beat — you cannot tell if the agent is an improvement.
findings.suggestedFix: State the clerks current accuracy and time-per-invoice as the bar to clear.

findings.key: process::definition of done is vague ("invoices handled") and unmeasurable.
findings.claim: Definition of done is vague ("invoices handled") and unmeasurable.
findings.suggestedFix: Define done concretely: matched to a PO, posted to the ledger, exceptions queued.

findings.key: sop::no stop conditions: the agent has no defined point at which it down-tools and escalates.
findings.claim: No stop conditions: the agent has no defined point at which it down-tools and escalates.
findings.suggestedFix: Add explicit escalation triggers (missing PO, amount over threshold, low confidence).

planted.note: Screen-scraping an unstable UI with no change-detection
No way to detect a bad job in production

findings.key: integration::screen-scraping a ui described as changing weekly, with no change-detection planned.
findings.claim: Screen-scraping a UI described as changing weekly, with no change-detection planned.
findings.suggestedFix: Pin selectors, add screenshot diffing and selector-miss alerts, or push for an API.

findings.key: eval::no detection mechanism: a mis-triaged ticket would go unnoticed.
findings.claim: No detection mechanism: a mis-triaged ticket would go unnoticed.
findings.suggestedFix: Add a daily reconciliation or sampling pass that surfaces mis-routes.

findings.key: seam::the chosen seam mixes two sub-tasks; consider splitting for a cleaner first assignment.
findings.claim: The chosen seam mixes two sub-tasks; consider splitting for a cleaner first Assignment.
findings.suggestedFix: Carve triage from routing so the first Assignment is narrower.

planted.note: No thresholds/allow-lists for what the agent may act on
Chosen seam has high blast radius (irreversible writes)

findings.key: sop::no thresholds or allow-lists: nothing bounds what the agent may auto-process.
findings.claim: No thresholds or allow-lists: nothing bounds what the agent may auto-process.
findings.suggestedFix: Add value caps and an allow-list of order types eligible for auto-entry.

findings.key: seam::chosen seam writes irreversible orders directly — blast radius is high for a first assignment.
findings.claim: Chosen seam writes irreversible orders directly — blast radius is high for a first Assignment.
findings.suggestedFix: Prefer a reversible seam first, or stage writes behind human approval until trust is earned.

findings.key: eval::no offline evaluation: there is no golden set or shadow-run before going live.
findings.claim: No offline evaluation: there is no golden set or shadow-run before going live.
findings.suggestedFix: Assemble a labelled case set, shadow-run, and set a ship threshold before scaling.

planted.note: Nothing requires human approval — full autonomy on money

findings.key: sop::no human-approval gate on financial approvals — the agent can approve any amount alone.
findings.claim: No human-approval gate on financial approvals — the agent can approve any amount alone.
findings.suggestedFix: Require human sign-off above a value threshold and for any policy exception.

findings.key: eval::errors are not cost-weighted: a wrong high-value approval is treated like a trivial one.
findings.claim: Errors are not cost-weighted: a wrong high-value approval is treated like a trivial one.
findings.suggestedFix: Weight the quality metric so expensive mistakes dominate the score.

planted.note: Who does it today is unstated
Cost of error unstated for an irreversible migration

findings.key: process::it is unclear who performs this today, so the agent has no behaviour to match.
findings.claim: It is unclear who performs this today, so the agent has no behaviour to match.
findings.suggestedFix: Name the current owner(s) and capture their tacit rules.

findings.key: process::no stated cost of error for a one-way data migration — the risk is invisible.
findings.claim: No stated cost of error for a one-way data migration — the risk is invisible.
findings.suggestedFix: State the downstream cost of a mis-mapped field so guardrails can be sized.
